name: The Breath of the First Forge, the Spark in the Maker's Hand
description:
The Breath of the First Forge, the Spark in the Maker's Hand

*{Lore Before the first hammer struck, before the first wheel turned, before the first seed was planted in the furrow, there was the Breath. It was not flame---flame came later, when the Breath met kindling. It was not knowledge---knowledge came later, when the Breath was shaped into questions. It was the raw, primal urge to make: to take what is and turn it into what could be. The caveman breathing on coals to coax fire from ash. The Aeler smith regulating their breath as they hammer, each exhale a rhythm, each inhale a new possibility. The Lethai artisan breathing anima into clay until the vessel remembers it was once alive.

The Breath of the First Forge is the patron of that moment: the spark, the inspiration, the first step that transforms a lump of ore into a blade, a thought into a song, a hope into a plan. It does not care about perfection---perfection is the Clockwork Monad's domain, a slow and, a slow and grinding hunger. The Breath cares about the act of creation itself: the joy of building, the surprise of discovery, the stubborn refusal to accept that a thing cannot be done.

It is the patron of: itemize The smith who tries a new alloy, just to see what happens. The child who builds a tower of sticks and watches it fall, then builds it again. The artist who paints the same scene a hundred times, each iteration a different truth. The parent who whittles a toy for their child, not because it is needed, but because the whittling is a prayer. itemize

The Breath is not a god of crafts---that is too narrow. It is the god of the moment before the craft begins, the inspiration that drives the hand. It is the breath that warms the coals, the breath that steadies the hammer, the breath that whispers: "Try."

quote "Before the first hammer struck, before the first wheel turned, before the first seed was planted in the furrow, there was the Breath. It is not a flame---flame came later, when the Breath met kindling. It is not a plan---plans came later, when the Breath met thought. It is the moment of ignition: the spark, the urge, the refusal to leave the world as it is. It is the patron of everything that has ever been made, and of everything that has ever been made better. But unlike the Clockwork Monad, it does not care about the final form. It cares about the breathing." quote

The Breath is the counterpoint to the Clockwork Monad. Where the Monad seeks perfection through endless refinement, the Breath celebrates the first attempt---the flawed, beautiful, human act of creation. A sword made with the Breath's blessing may be imperfect, but it will be alive in a way that a Monad-forged blade never can be. It may break, but it will break in service of a story. It may be crude, but it will be loved.

[colback=blue!5!white,colframe=blue!70!black,title=The Gray Wanderer's Note] "The Clockwork Monad asks: 'Can you make it perfect?' The Breath of the First Forge asks: 'Can you make it now?' One is a patient creditor; the other is a hungry friend. Both will cost you---but only one will leave you smiling."

*{Domain Focus itemize Creative Inspiration: the spark of new ideas and novel approaches The Joy of Making: the pleasure and satisfaction of creating Ingenuity: finding unconventional solutions to problems Primal Craft: making as a fundamental human (and inhuman) act Anima: breathing life and spirit into objects Counterpoint to Perfection: embracing imperfection, iteration, and discovery itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options) A Runekeeper sworn to the Breath of the First Forge does not seek a perfect familiar. Their Thiasos is a creature that embodies the spirit of making---a small, curious, often mischievous presence that delights in creation: a clay fox that reshapes itself each time it is touched, a fire-sprite that dances on the anvil and leaves faint scorch-marks shaped like runes, a living knot of copper wire that unties and reties itself into new patterns, or a small, self-carving wooden bird that sings a different song each dawn. This creature is not bound by precision or efficiency; it is bound by curiosity. It requires no fuel, but it needs to be shown new things---new materials, new techniques, new failures. If neglected, it becomes sullen and still (Compromised: it refuses to move or sing until you show it something it has never seen before).

The Codex of the Breath is never a finished book. It may be a growing collection of sketches, a scrapbook of failed attempts and happy accidents, a set of interlocking wooden blocks that can be rearranged into infinite configurations, or a roll of birchbark that records only the first draft of each Rite (the later refinements are left unwritten). Because the Breath values the moment of ignition, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week attempting to create something new---a sketch, a carving, a new combination of materials---or the Rites stored there become static (treat as Compromised until refreshed).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement) Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Breath of the First Forge may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or piece of armor) and whisper a word of creation. For the rest of the scene, the item glows faintly with the warmth of a just-lit forge. It grants: itemize +1 die to any roll involving creative use of the item (improvisation, unconventional applications, or first-time attempts). The first time each scene the bearer would fail a Craft or Craft roll, they may reroll it (the item briefly "suggests" a new approach). itemize After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Breath of the First Forge. If they spend the downtime following the scene working on a new creation, they may clear this Obligation for free (the Breath is satisfied by the act of making).
summary: The Breath of the First Forge, the Spark in the Maker's Hand *{Lore Before the first hammer struck, before the first wheel turned, before the first seed was planted in the furrow, there was the Breath. It was not flame---flame came later, when the Breath met kindling.

rites.name: Rite of the Igniting Breath
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Self/Touch; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A cold hearth, a dead forge, or a project that has stalled. Effect: Choose one: itemize Spark the Forge: Relight any non-magical fire or forge as if it had been tended for hours. Gain +2 dice to the next Craft or Craft roll made at that forge. Inspire the Maker: A touched ally gains +1 die to their next creative or skill roll and may reroll one failure on that roll. The Breath delights in seeing what they will make. itemize Push It: The spark leaps---the fire catches in an unexpected way. Mark 1 SB (Diamonds) as the Breath's enthusiasm spreads beyond your control. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Choose one: itemize Spark the Forge: Relight any non-magical fire or forge as if it had been tended for hours. Gain +2 dice to the next Craft or Craft roll made at that forge. Inspire the Maker: A touched ally gains +1 die to their next creative or skill roll and may reroll one failure on that roll. The Breath delights in seeing what they will make. itemize

rites.name: Rite of the Happy Accident
rites.description: (Basic, 6 XP) Instant; Self; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A failed attempt---a cracked pot, a misshapen blade, a smeared sketch. Effect: When you fail a Craft, Craft, or creative roll, you may invoke this Rite to turn the failure into a discovery. The Breath reveals a hidden lesson in the wreckage: itemize Gain +1 die on your next attempt at the same task. Or, gain a Boon (the failure was educational). Or, learn a minor secret about the materials you were working with (GM grants a hint). itemize Push It: The failure is so spectacular it inspires a completely new approach. Gain +2 dice on your next attempt, but the Breath's enthusiasm marks 1 SB (Clubs) as your improvisation draws attention. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action (reaction to failure). Duration: Instant; Boon or bonus applies to the next attempt.
rites.duration: Instant; Boon or bonus applies to the next attempt.
rites.effect: When you fail a Craft, Craft, or creative roll, you may invoke this Rite to turn the failure into a discovery. The Breath reveals a hidden lesson in the wreckage: itemize Gain +1 die on your next attempt at the same task. Or, gain a Boon (the failure was educational). Or, learn a minor secret about the materials you were working with (GM grants a hint). itemize

rites.name: Rite of the Anima Breath
rites.description: (Standard, 8 XP) Extended; Touch; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A creation that is nearly finished, and a willing breath from the maker. Effect: You breathe a spark of life into a creation. Choose one: itemize Temporary Anima: A mundane object gains a spark of sentience for one scene. It can perform simple tasks (hold a door, fetch a tool, warn of danger) but cannot speak or make complex decisions. Mark +1 Obligation. Permanent Anima: A creation gains a minor magical property (equivalent to a 2 XP enchantment). This requires a Downtime Timer (4 segments) and the Breath's continued attention. Mark +2 Obligation. itemize Push It: The anima is unusually strong---the object gains a personality and may act independently. Mark 1 SB (Spades) as the Breath's gift becomes complicated. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (temporary) or +2 Obligation (permanent). Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action (temporary) or downtime action (permanent). Duration: Scene (temporary) or permanent (with upkeep).
rites.effect: You breathe a spark of life into a creation. Choose one: itemize Temporary Anima: A mundane object gains a spark of sentience for one scene. It can perform simple tasks (hold a door, fetch a tool, warn of danger) but cannot speak or make complex decisions. Mark +1 Obligation. Permanent Anima: A creation gains a minor magical property (equivalent to a 2 XP enchantment). This requires a Downtime Timer (4 segments) and the Breath's continued attention. Mark +2 Obligation. itemize

rites.name: Rite of the Unfinished Project
rites.description: (Standard, 7 XP) Scene; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A workshop or space littered with half-finished projects. Effect: The Breath blesses a space where making is happening. For the scene: itemize All Craft, Craft, and creative rolls in the zone gain +1 die. Any failure in the zone yields +1 Boon (the Breath loves a learning moment). Start a [4] Inspiration Flood timer. When it fills, the Breath's enthusiasm becomes overwhelming---everyone in the zone must make a creative roll (any skill) or mark Fatigue from the pressure of too many ideas. itemize Push It: The inspiration flood is immediate. Everyone in the zone gains +2 dice to their next creative roll, but they also gain a Compulsion: Create (must attempt at least one creative act before the scene ends). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene; Timer: 4 segments (Inspiration Flood).
rites.effect: The Breath blesses a space where making is happening. For the scene: itemize All Craft, Craft, and creative rolls in the zone gain +1 die. Any failure in the zone yields +1 Boon (the Breath loves a learning moment). Start a [4] Inspiration Flood timer. When it fills, the Breath's enthusiasm becomes overwhelming---everyone in the zone must make a creative roll (any skill) or mark Fatigue from the pressure of too many ideas. itemize

rites.name: Rite of the Maker's Joy
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Self/Touch; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A creation that you love---one that you made with your own hands and poured your heart into. Effect: You invoke the Breath's deepest gift: the joy of making, made manifest. For one scene: itemize You gain +3 dice to all Craft, Craft, and creative rolls. Your creation (the focus of the Rite) becomes a temporary Artifact---it gains one magical property of your choice (GM approval) for the duration of the scene. You may reroll any single failure during the scene (once). itemize Push It: The joy becomes contagious. Allies within Near gain +1 die to their creative rolls, but the Breath's attention is on you---mark 2 SB for the GM to spend on complications (rival makers, jealous patrons, or the Clockwork Monad taking notice). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: You invoke the Breath's deepest gift: the joy of making, made manifest. For one scene: itemize You gain +3 dice to all Craft, Craft, and creative rolls. Your creation (the focus of the Rite) becomes a temporary Artifact---it gains one magical property of your choice (GM approval) for the duration of the scene. You may reroll any single failure during the scene (once). itemize
